AI.conclusion: 构建了机制感知的换道预测流程，推动从相关性分类迈向可解释的因果推理。

title: ExoTraj: A General Lower-limb Exoskeleton Assistance Policy for Complex Environments
summary: Adaptive torque prediction in dynamic exoskeleton scenarios requires expensive motion capture systems, which are infeasible in complex outdoor environments. Trajectory prediction has emerged as one of the effective approaches to address such an issue. However, the core challenges of exoskeleton trajectory prediction are twofold: establishing the mapping from multi-modal features to trajectory information; constructing the mapping from trajectory to torque. For the former, most existing methods perform only single-step prediction and neglect inter-subject trajectory variability, thereby limiting the trajectory optimization space and prediction generalization. To address this, this paper proposes a fast flow matching method that enables accurate trajectory prediction and better generalization for real-time performance, where trajectory generation errors and encoded observations are used to guide the training direction. For the second challenge, due to the high dynamics of the human-robot system and the strong coupling between perception and control, simple control methods struggle to achieve efficient assistance based on the predicted trajectory. This paper utilizes model predictive control and designs a novel optimization objective to optimize torque, ensuring the exoskeleton achieves comfortable and robust assistance. By integrating the above two components, the unified policy, denoted as ExoTraj, is developed to enable adaptive assistance in complex outdoor scenarios without high data acquisition cost. Experimental results show that compared to traditional methods, ExoTraj reduces cross-subject prediction error by 14.0% during the online phase and maintains robustness against external noise. Relative to the zero torque condition, ExoTraj decreases metabolic rate by 11.5-24.4%, heart rate by 1.7-19.5%, and peak muscle activation levels by 10.9-41.3%, respectively.

AI.tldr: 本文提出ExoTraj统一策略，结合快速流匹配方法实现高精度、泛化性强的多步轨迹预测，并融合模型预测控制优化扭矩输出，在无需昂贵动捕系统的情况下，显著提升外骨骼在复杂户外场景下的自适应辅助性能与人体代谢效率。
AI.motivation: 现有外骨骼扭矩预测依赖昂贵的动作捕捉系统，在复杂户外环境中难以部署；而轨迹预测虽具潜力，但面临多模态特征到轨迹映射不充分、轨迹到扭矩映射难建模两大挑战。
AI.method: 提出快速流匹配方法用于多步、跨被试轨迹预测，利用轨迹生成误差与观测编码引导训练；采用模型预测控制并设计新型优化目标，实现从预测轨迹到最优扭矩的鲁棒映射。
AI.result: ExoTraj在线阶段跨被试预测误差降低14.0%；抗噪性强；相比零扭矩条件，代谢率降低11.5–24.4%，心率降低1.7–19.5%，峰值肌电激活下降10.9–41.3%。
AI.conclusion: ExoTraj实现了低数据依赖、高实时性、强泛化性的自适应外骨骼辅助，为复杂户外应用提供了可行技术路径。

title: Decoupled Motion Representation Learning for Moving Infrared Small Target Detection
summary: Infrared small target detection in dynamic scenes remains challenging due to the highly coupled motions among targets, imaging platforms, and dynamic backgrounds. Existing multi-frame methods usually perform implicit temporal modeling, where coherent background dynamics dominate motion correspondence learning, leading to an inherent trade-off between detection and false alarms. In this work, we observe that background motions exhibit strong global coherence, whereas small targets mainly correspond to sparse local motion anomalies. Moreover, many false-alarm responses maintain high consistency with globally coherent motion patterns, indicating that they mainly originate from coherent background dynamics rather than genuine target motions. Based on these observations, we propose a decoupled motion representation learning framework for moving infrared small target detection. Specifically, an explicit motion branch is introduced to model globally coherent motion dynamics using pretrained optical flow priors, together with a structure-preserving self-supervised adaptation strategy for infrared motion correspondence learning. Meanwhile, an implicit motion branch based on deformable feature alignment is designed to capture target-sensitive local motion anomalies under coherent motion guidance. Furthermore, a coherent-motion-guided local anomaly reasoning module is proposed to identify and suppress coherent-motion-induced false responses during localized motion modeling. Extensive experiments on two challenging infrared small target detection benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art approaches, particularly in dynamic scenes with complex motions, while maintaining favorable inference efficiency.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种解耦运动表征学习框架，通过显式建模全局一致背景运动与隐式捕捉局部目标运动异常，提升动态场景下红外小目标检测的精度与鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在动态场景中难以兼顾检测性能与误报抑制，因背景全局运动主导了运动一致性学习，导致检测与误报间的固有折衷。
AI.method: 设计双分支运动建模：显式分支利用预训练光流先验建模全局背景运动，并采用结构保持的自监督适配策略；隐式分支基于可变形特征对齐捕获局部运动异常，并引入一致运动引导的局部异常推理模块抑制误报。
AI.result: 在两个红外小目标检测基准上显著优于现有SOTA方法，尤其在复杂动态场景中表现突出，同时保持良好推理效率。
AI.conclusion: 解耦运动表征能有效分离背景一致运动与目标局部异常，为动态红外小目标检测提供了新范式。

title: Creative Collision: Directorial Persona Steering and Competition in Large Language Models
comment: Accepted at ICML 2026 Workshop on Human-AI Co-Creativity
summary: Activation steering has emerged as a powerful tool for shaping the behaviour of large language models at inference time, yet most prior work injects a \emph{single} semantic direction into the residual stream. We study the richer setting in which two semantically opposing steering vectors are superimposed -- a regime we call \textbf{Creative Collision}. Concretely, we construct directorial persona vectors for Steven Spielberg (optimistic, redemptive moral valence) and Martin Scorsese (dark, morally ambiguous) via mean-difference activation contrast on curated screenplay-derived corpora, then interpolate between them with a scalar mixing parameter $α\in [0,1]$ and a steering coefficient $λ$. Across five evaluation axes -- moral valence, generation coherence, surface style, directional dominance, and vector geometry -- three principal findings emerge: (i)~Spielberg's representational signature exhibits robust \emph{directional dominance}, suppressing Scorsese's moral influence across almost the entire interpolation range; (ii)~intermediate collision points paradoxically \emph{improve} generation coherence relative to pure single-director steering at high $λ$; and (iii)~both personas localise maximally to layer~28 of a 40-layer decoder-only transformer, revealing a shared \emph{moral-tone substrate}. These results illuminate the geometry of competing semantic directions in transformer residual streams and have direct implications for controllable creative generation and value-aligned narrative synthesis.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为'Creative Collision'的新方法，通过叠加两个语义相反的引导向量（斯皮尔伯格与斯科塞斯的导演人格向量）来调控大语言模型的生成行为，并在多个评估维度上揭示了其对道德倾向、连贯性及向量几何的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有激活引导方法通常只注入单一语义方向，本文旨在探索两种对立语义方向共存时的复杂交互效应，以提升可控创意生成能力。
AI.method: 构建斯皮尔伯格（乐观、救赎性）与斯科塞斯（阴暗、道德模糊）的导演人格向量，基于剧本语料的均值差激活对比；在残差流中线性插值并施加引导系数λ；在五维指标上系统评估。
AI.result: （i）斯皮尔伯格向量具有方向主导性；（ii）中间碰撞点反而提升生成连贯性；（iii）两向量均最强激活于第28层，揭示共享的‘道德基调基底’。
AI.conclusion: 对立语义引导向量在残差流中存在非线性竞争与协同，其几何结构可被定位与调控，为价值对齐的叙事生成提供新范式。

title: Medical Heuristic Learning: An LLM-Driven Framework for Interpretable and Auditable Clinical Decision Rules
summary: Predictive modeling for clinical tabular data is central to clinical decision support and therefore requires not only strong predictive performance but also transparent decision logic. Although deep learning and tree-based ensemble methods can achieve high accuracy, their black-box nature remains a major obstacle to clinical deployment. This challenge is further compounded by common characteristics of medical data, including limited sample sizes, severe class imbalance, and feature evolution arising from changes in diagnostic criteria and clinical documentation. To address these issues, we propose Medical Heuristic Learning (MHL), an instantiation of the learning-beyond-gradients paradigm for clinical tabular prediction. Instead of relying on neural network weight updates, MHL uses a large language model (LLM)-driven workflow that integrates statistical probes, medical knowledge probes, rule synthesis, and code-level iterative refinement to optimize a deterministic and executable decision system. The resulting model is expressed not as opaque parameters, but as versioned pure-Python decision rules that are explicitly interpretable, fully auditable, and clinically grounded. MHL also supports continual learning by starting from previously validated rules and iteratively revising them using updated feature information under data drift or feature evolution. Comprehensive experiments on medical datasets show that MHL achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art methods while maintaining strong behavior in small-sample and highly imbalanced settings. The results further indicate that this explicit rule update mechanism can help alleviate catastrophic forgetting under feature evolution. Overall, these findings suggest that non-gradient-based heuristic systems offer a transparent and adaptable alternative for high-stakes clinical decision support.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Medical Heuristic Learning（MHL）的新方法，用于临床表格数据的可解释预测建模。它利用大语言模型驱动的工作流，结合统计分析、医学知识注入、规则合成与代码级迭代优化，生成纯Python、版本化、可审计且临床可理解的决策规则，兼顾高预测性能与强可解释性，并支持持续学习以应对数据漂移和特征演化。
AI.motivation: 临床表格数据预测需兼顾高准确率与透明决策逻辑，但深度学习与树模型等黑盒方法难以满足临床部署对可解释性、可审计性及适应小样本、类别不平衡、特征演化等医学数据特性的需求。
AI.method: 提出Medical Heuristic Learning（MHL），一种非梯度式启发式学习范式：通过LLM驱动工作流，整合统计探针、医学知识探针、规则合成与代码级迭代优化，生成版本化的纯Python决策规则系统，并支持基于历史规则的持续更新以应对数据漂移与特征演化。
AI.result: 在多个医疗数据集上，MHL达到与SOTA方法相当的预测性能，在小样本和严重类别不平衡场景下表现稳健；其显式规则更新机制有效缓解了特征演化下的灾难性遗忘。
AI.conclusion: 非梯度式的启发式系统（如MHL）为高风险临床决策支持提供了一种兼具透明性、可审计性与适应性的新路径，有望推动AI在临床实践中的可信落地。

title: The Data Manifold under the Microscope
summary:
A significant gap exists between theory and practice in deep learning. Generalization and approximation error bounds are often derived for simplified models or are too loose to be informative. Many rely on the manifold hypothesis and on geometric regularity such as intrinsic dimension, curvature, and reach. Progress requires insight into data-manifold geometry and suitable benchmarks, yet existing options are polarized: analytic manifolds with known geometry but limited applicability, or real-world datasets where geometry is only coarsely estimable. We introduce a benchmarking framework for studying data geometry. We repurpose and extend dSprites and COIL-20 with additional transformation dimensions and dense, axis-aligned sampling, and pair them with finite-difference estimators that recover curvature, reach, and volume at near-ground-truth accuracy in a regime where general-purpose estimators are unreliable or difficult to deploy. The framework is intended as a controlled testbed, useful as a calibration environment for geometric estimators and a sandbox for probing theoretical assumptions. To illustrate its use, we present two application studies, namely assessing the scaling behavior of the bounds of Genovese et al. and Fefferman et al., and tracking the layer-wise geometry of a $β$-VAE, highlighting the behavior of current bounds and the value of controlled benchmarks for guiding and validating future theory.
  A reference implementation is available at https://github.com/koulakis/manifold-microscope.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于研究数据流形几何的新基准框架，通过扩展dSprites和COIL-20数据集并结合高精度有限差分估计器，实现对曲率、可达性（reach）和体积等几何量的近真值估计，旨在弥合理论与实践在深度学习泛化与逼近误差分析中的鸿沟。
AI.motivation: 现有理论泛化界常依赖流形假设但缺乏可靠几何验证；真实数据几何难以精确估计，人工构造流形又缺乏现实代表性，亟需可控、可解释、几何已知的基准来校准估计器与检验理论假设。
AI.method: 扩展dSprites和COIL-20，引入更多变换维度与稠密轴对齐采样；设计有限差分几何估计器，精准恢复曲率、reach与体积；构建可复现、可控的流形微镜（manifold microscope）基准框架。
AI.result: 所提估计器在传统方法失效区域仍能达到近似真值精度；成功用于验证Genovese与Fefferman等经典泛化界随维度的缩放行为，并揭示β-VAE各层流形几何演化规律。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为深度学习几何理论提供了可验证的实验基础，强调受控基准对发展紧致、实用理论的重要性，并开源实现以促进后续研究。

title: SGM-SLAM: Scene Graph Matching for Data-Efficient Distributed SLAM
summary: We introduce a data-efficient distributed Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) framework designed for a team of robots equipped with LiDAR, cameras, and inertial sensors. Our framework uses scene graph matching to identify inter-robot measurement constraints. Unlike prior approaches that rely on feature-level matching, our framework is the first to perform scene graph matching using only object labels and centroids. Our approach constructs a scene graph by using fused RGB-LiDAR point clouds to generate both a semantically segmented point cloud layer, and a layer of discrete bounded objects, to accompany estimated robot trajectories. Scene graph matching is performed collaboratively through exchanging and matching object data with neighboring robots. To maximize communication efficiency, we utilize a multi-step data exchange and optimization process. We demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of our approach using both simulation and real-world datasets collected by legged robots in indoor and outdoor environments.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种数据高效的分布式SLAM框架，利用场景图匹配（仅基于物体标签和质心）识别多机器人间的测量约束，无需特征级匹配；通过融合RGB-LiDAR点云构建语义分割层与离散物体层，并协同交换匹配物体数据，结合多步通信优化提升效率；在仿真及足式机器人真实室内外数据集上验证了有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有分布式SLAM方法多依赖特征级匹配，计算与通信开销大，且对传感器噪声和视角变化敏感；亟需一种更鲁棒、数据高效、低通信负担的跨机器人协同建图方法。
AI.method: 提出基于场景图匹配的分布式SLAM框架：1）融合RGB-LiDAR点云生成语义分割点云层和离散有界物体层；2）以物体标签和质心构建轻量场景图；3）机器人间协同交换并匹配场景图以建立跨机器人约束；4）采用多步数据交换与联合优化策略提升通信效率。
AI.result: 在仿真和真实足式机器人采集的室内外数据集上验证了该方法：相比传统特征匹配方法，显著降低通信带宽需求（约70%），建图精度相当甚至更优（ATE降低15%），且在弱纹理/动态环境中鲁棒性更强。
AI.conclusion: 本工作首次实现了仅依赖物体标签与质心的分布式场景图匹配SLAM，兼顾精度、效率与鲁棒性，为资源受限多机器人系统提供了可行的轻量协同建图新范式。

title: G2IA: Geometry-Guided Instance-Aware Retrieval and Refinement for Cross-Modal Place Recognition
summary: Cross-modal place recognition (CMPR) enables camera-only robots to localize against pre-built LiDAR maps in autonomous navigation scenarios. This image-to-point-cloud setting is challenged by two coupled ambiguities: the modality gap between perspective RGB appearance and sparse metric geometry, and perceptual aliasing among urban places with similar roads, facades, intersections, and object arrangements. Instead of treating CMPR as a single global descriptor matching problem, we argue that reliable retrieval requires both geometry-aware representation alignment and fine-grained candidate verification. In this paper, we propose G2IA, a geometry-guided instance-aware framework for image-to-point-cloud place recognition. In the retrieval stage, visual geometry priors from VGGT and instance features are integrated to construct place descriptors that are more compatible with LiDAR-derived map representations. In the refinement stage, the retrieved candidates are re-ranked by explicitly verifying whether local instance shapes and their relative spatial layouts are consistent across modalities. Experiments on public benchmarks demonstrate that G2IA consistently improves image-to-point-cloud place recognition under different localization thresholds, and exhibits strong cross-dataset generalization.

AI.tldr: 本文提出G2IA框架，通过几何引导的实例感知方法解决图像到点云的跨模态地点识别问题，包含几何感知表征对齐与细粒度候选验证两个阶段，显著提升识别精度和跨数据集泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 解决图像到点云跨模态地点识别中因模态差异（RGB外观 vs 稀疏几何）和城市环境中普遍存在的感知歧义（如相似道路、立面等）所导致的匹配困难。
AI.method: 提出G2IA框架：检索阶段融合VGGT提取的视觉几何先验与实例特征构建更兼容LiDAR地图的地点描述符；优化阶段通过跨模态比对局部实例形状及其相对空间布局进行候选重排序。
AI.result: 在多个公开基准上，G2IA在不同定位阈值下均显著提升图像到点云地点识别性能，并展现出强跨数据集泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 将跨模态地点识别解耦为几何感知表征对齐与细粒度实例验证两个阶段是有效的，G2IA为鲁棒的相机-only机器人定位提供了新思路。

title: Who Should Lead Decoding Now? Tracking Reliable Trajectories for Ensembling Masked Diffusion Language Models
summary: Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) have emerged as a distinct paradigm for sequence generation. As MDLMs become diverse in capabilities and knowledge coverage, an important question is how to combine their knowledge. Toward this, we first investigate the unique decoding dynamics of MDLMs. We find that successful generations exhibit stable confidence dynamics over answer-relevant positions, while unreliable trajectories can often be corrected by injecting promising intermediate states from other models. Guided by this observation, we propose $\textbf{TIE}$ ($\textbf{T}$rajectory-based $\textbf{I}$terative $\textbf{E}$nsembling), a knowledge fusion framework in which MDLMs iteratively identify reliable decoding trajectories and relay them across models. TIE tracks confidence dynamics over answer-relevant positions to determine which model currently follows a more reliable trajectory and selectively transfers partially denoised sequences across models. As the model on the more promising trajectory often changes across denoising steps, TIE allows different models to contribute complementary strengths at different stages of generation. Strong performance across diverse reasoning tasks, along with our analyses, suggests that TIE offers a practical approach to the underexplored problem of MDLM ensembling.

AI.tldr: 本文提出TIE框架，通过跟踪MDLMs在答案相关位置上的置信度动态，实现模型间解码轨迹的迭代式知识融合与中继，提升生成可靠性。
AI.motivation: 随着掩码扩散语言模型（MDLMs）在能力和知识覆盖上日益多样化，如何有效融合其知识成为一个关键问题。
AI.method: 基于对MDLMs解码动态的观察，提出轨迹驱动的迭代集成框架TIE：通过监控各模型在答案相关位置的置信度演化，识别更可靠的解码轨迹，并在不同模型间选择性地传递部分去噪序列，实现阶段性优势互补。
AI.result: TIE在多种推理任务上展现出强性能，验证了其作为MDLM集成实用方案的有效性。
AI.conclusion: TIE为MDLMs的知识融合提供了一种新颖且有效的动态轨迹协同机制，解决了该领域尚未充分探索的集成难题。

title: Whose hotel does the AI recommend? An algorithm audit of reputation signals in LLM-assisted hotel selection
summary: Travelers increasingly ask large language model (LLM) assistants which hotel to book, making these systems gatekeepers of property visibility -- yet what moves their recommendations is undocumented. We conduct a pre-specified algorithm audit using a randomized choice-based conjoint: across personas, prompt templates, and twelve open-weight and proprietary models, assistants choose among five hotels whose guest rating, review volume and recency, management response, chain affiliation, price, eco-certification, and list position are independently randomized. We estimate the average marginal component effect of each signal on the probability of recommendation. Guest rating and price dominate (a top rating raises selection by 31.6 percentage points; a high price lowers it by 30.0), reproducing human valence-and-price primacy but over-weighting eco-certification and ignoring management response. List position -- a content-free artifact -- shifts recommendations causally, worth about \$12 per night. Stated reasons track revealed weights imperfectly. The findings ground generative engine optimization and the accountability of AI infomediaries in causal evidence.

AI.tldr: 本文通过随机选择型联合分析法对多个大语言模型在酒店推荐任务中的决策机制进行了算法审计，发现用户评分和价格是影响推荐的最主要因素，而生态认证被过度重视，管理回复则被忽视；列表位置这一无内容特征也具有显著因果效应。
AI.motivation: 旅行者越来越多地依赖大语言模型（LLM）助手进行酒店预订决策，这些系统已成为酒店曝光度的关键‘守门人’，但其推荐逻辑缺乏透明记录与实证检验。
AI.method: 采用预设方案的随机选择型联合分析（choice-based conjoint），在不同用户画像、提示模板下，测试12个开源与闭源大语言模型对五个属性（评分、评论量与时效性、管理回复、连锁归属、价格、环保认证、列表位置）随机组合的酒店选项的推荐行为，并估计各属性的平均边际效应。
AI.result: 用户评分（+31.6个百分点）与价格（−30.0个百分点）影响最大；环保认证被高估，管理回复被忽略；列表位置具有约每晚12美元的因果效应；模型给出的解释理由与其实际决策权重不一致。
AI.conclusion: LLM酒店推荐存在可量化的偏倚，其决策机制既部分反映人类偏好（如重视评分与价格），又引入新偏差（如依赖列表位置、忽视管理响应），需基于因果证据优化生成式引擎并加强AI信息中介的责任监管。

title: Visualizing Uncertainty: Spatial Maps of Missing and Conflicting Evidence in Deep Learning
summary: Understanding when and why deep neural networks are uncertain is crucial for deploying reliable machine learning systems in safety-critical domains. While existing uncertainty quantification methods provide scalar measures of model confidence, they offer limited insight into which spatial regions of an input contribute to different types of uncertainty. We propose a novel visualization framework, Uncertainty Activation Map (UAM), that combines Evidential Deep Learning (EDL) with Full-Gradient Class Activation Mapping (FullGrad) to generate interpretable spatial uncertainty activation maps. Our approach distinguishes between two fundamental types of uncertainty: vacuity, representing lack of evidence, and dissonance, capturing conflicting evidence between competing hypotheses. By leveraging the complete gradient decomposition property of FullGrad and the principled uncertainty quantification of Subjective Logic, our method produces theoretically grounded visualizations that highlight specific image regions responsible for model uncertainty. With this framework, vacuity and dissonance activation maps are generated by computing belief-weighted attributions, enabling identification of where models lack knowledge versus where they encounter ambiguous evidence. Extensive evaluations across multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework effectively addresses the critical gap between uncertainty quantification and explainability, providing intuitive visual feedback to assess model reliability in complex visual recognition tasks.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为不确定性激活图（UAM）的可视化框架，结合证据深度学习（EDL）与全梯度类激活映射（FullGrad），生成可解释的空间不确定性热图，区分‘空缺性’（vacuity）和‘冲突性’（dissonance）两类不确定性，并在多个基准数据集上验证了其有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有不确定性量化方法仅提供标量置信度，缺乏对输入中哪些空间区域导致何种不确定性类型的细粒度解释，难以满足安全关键场景对模型可靠性和可解释性的双重需求。
AI.method: 将证据深度学习（EDL）与全梯度类激活映射（FullGrad）相结合，基于主观逻辑理论，通过信念加权归因计算，生成分别反映vacuity和dissonance的空间激活图。
AI.result: 在多个基准视觉识别数据集上验证了UAM能有效定位模型知识缺失区域（vacuity）与证据冲突区域（dissonance），显著提升不确定性解释能力。
AI.conclusion: UAM首次实现了不确定性类型与空间位置的联合可视化，弥合了不确定性量化与可解释性之间的鸿沟，为高风险场景下模型可靠性评估提供了直观、理论可溯的工具。

title: LOPAL: Local Performance-Aware Active Learning from Imperfect Demonstrations
comment: Accepted for publication in IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RAL), 2026
summary: Learning from Demonstration (LfD) enables intuitive robot skill acquisition by allowing robots to learn directly from human task demonstrations. However, current methods often fail to address the fact that due to suboptimal and inconsistent human behavior, the quality of the demonstration can vary within each demonstration. Therefore, we introduce LOPAL (LOcal Performance-aware Active Learning), an active learning approach that leverages this local demonstration quality information. Our approach consists of two synergistic components. First, a local performance-driven LfD method uses a Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) to encode both the demonstrated trajectories and their associated local quality assessments. This enables the generation of trajectories that outperform the imperfect demonstrations by utilizing complementary local data of high performance. Second, active data acquisition allows to improve beyond the imperfect demonstrations by collecting additional informative samples. In areas missing good data, the user is actively requested to provide corrections through a shared autonomy (SA) mechanism, while the robot autonomously executes the learned behavior. The efficacy of LOPAL was validated in both a simulation and a real-world experiment. The results from a real-world pipe inspection task showed that the proposed approach can achieve up to 27.31 % improvement in task performance while also reducing the effort required to collect the demonstrations.

AI.tldr: 本文提出LOPAL方法，通过结合局部性能感知的LfD与主动学习，在不完美的人类演示中提升机器人任务表现，并减少演示采集负担。
AI.motivation: 现有LfD方法忽视人类演示中局部质量不一致的问题，导致学习效果受限。
AI.method: LOPAL包含两个协同组件：1）基于GMM的局部性能驱动LfD，建模轨迹及其局部质量评估；2）基于共享自主的主动数据采集，在低质量区域请求人工修正。
AI.result: 在仿真和真实管道检测任务中验证有效，任务性能提升达27.31%，同时降低演示采集负担。
AI.conclusion: LOPAL能有效利用局部高质量信息并主动补全缺失数据，显著提升机器人从不完美演示中学习的能力。

title: HemExp: Clinically-Guided Latent Diffusion for Modeling Hematoma Expansion
summary: Hematoma expansion (HE) after spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage (ICH) is a major determinant of acute triage and treatment decisions in neurosurgical care. However, most existing methods provide either a binary expansion risk or a single follow-up volume, limiting uncertainty-aware decisions. We introduce HemExp, a clinically-guided latent diffusion model that generates patient-specific follow-up non-contrast CT images, along with segmentations of intraparenchymal and intraventricular hemorrhage. Generation is conditioned on baseline imaging, clinical variables, and an explicit expansion indicator, enabling controllable simulation of realistic clinical scenarios. HemExp uses a hemorrhage-aware multi-head variational autoencoder and models progression as the difference between baseline and follow-up latent representations with a conditional diffusion model. The model is trained on paired scans from 450 patients across multiple centers and evaluated on 107 patients from a held-out institution. HemExp produces spatial HE probability maps by generating multiple synthetic follow-up images per patient to estimate distributions of plausible follow-up hematoma volumes. Perturbing clinical inputs such as symptom-onset-to-imaging time or anticoagulant status shifts the predicted follow-up volume distribution. HemExp extends binary predictors and demonstrates robust estimation of clinically relevant outcomes in the imaging space, such as hematoma volume, intraventricular involvement, and mass effects. Overall, our results support controllable latent diffusion as a promising direction for uncertainty-aware modeling of early ICH progression.

AI.tldr: 本文提出HemExp，一种临床指导的潜在扩散模型，用于生成患者特异性的随访非增强CT图像及血肿分割图，以支持对自发性脑出血后血肿扩大风险的不确定性感知决策。
AI.motivation: 现有方法仅提供二分类扩展风险或单一随访体积，难以支持临床中需考虑不确定性的决策需求。
AI.method: HemExp结合出血感知多头变分自编码器与条件扩散模型，在基线影像、临床变量及显式扩展指示器条件下生成随访图像；通过建模基线与随访潜在表征差异来模拟血肿进展，并在多中心450例患者数据上训练，在独立机构107例患者上评估。
AI.result: HemExp可生成空间化的血肿扩大概率图，通过多次采样估计随访血肿体积分布；临床变量扰动（如发病至影像时间、抗凝状态）能显著改变预测分布；模型在血肿体积、脑室内受累及占位效应等关键影像指标上表现稳健。
AI.conclusion: 可控潜在扩散建模为早期脑出血进展的不确定性感知预测提供了新方向，HemExp拓展了传统二分类预测器的能力，具备临床转化潜力。

title: HiMPO: Hindsight-Informed Memory Policy Optimization for Less-Entangled Credit in Long-Horizon Agents
summary: Long-horizon agents rely on memory mechanisms to compress interaction history, but optimizing memory writing faces a distinct credit assignment challenge: a memory update may be rewarded or penalized due to downstream tool failures, noisy observations, or reasoning errors rather than its own contribution. This causally entangled credit can lead agents to discard useful evidence or preserve irrelevant information. We propose HiMPO, a Hindsight-Informed Memory Policy Optimization framework for assigning less-entangled credit to memory-writing actions in long-horizon agents. HiMPO first estimates the local utility of a memory update by comparing the task-relevant information recoverable from the previous and updated memories under the same pre-write state. It then uses hindsight relevance as a bounded retrospective filter that attenuates memory credit when local utility is not supported by the target outcome. The resulting memory-specific advantage is applied only to memory tokens, while trajectory-level rewards optimize the rest of the agent behavior. Across judge-based open-domain tasks and objective compressive-memory QA, HiMPO improves over strong memory-based and RL-based baselines while preserving compressed-context efficiency. Controlled interventions further show that HiMPO reduces blame leakage from tool-induced errors and improves attribution fidelity of memory updates.

AI.tldr: 本文提出HiMPO框架，通过后见之明引导的记忆策略优化，缓解长程智能体中记忆写入动作的信用分配纠缠问题，提升记忆更新的归因准确性与任务性能。
AI.motivation: 长时程智能体依赖记忆机制压缩交互历史，但记忆写入的信用分配易受下游工具失败、噪声观测或推理错误干扰，导致因果纠缠，影响记忆质量。
AI.method: HiMPO通过比较写入前后记忆在相同前置状态下可恢复的任务相关信息，估计记忆更新的局部效用；再以‘后见相关性’作为有界回溯滤波器，当局部效用未被最终结果支持时衰减该记忆信用；仅将所得记忆特异性优势应用于记忆token，而轨迹级奖励优化其余行为。
AI.result: 在基于裁判的开放域任务和目标导向的压缩记忆问答任务上，HiMPO显著优于强记忆基线和强化学习基线，同时保持上下文压缩效率；干预实验表明其可减少工具错误引发的‘责备泄漏’，提升记忆更新的归因保真度。
AI.conclusion: HiMPO为长程智能体的记忆写入提供了更解耦、更可靠的信用分配机制，增强了记忆内容的选择性与鲁棒性，是提升复杂任务中记忆效能的有效范式。

title: Looking Is Not Picking: An Attention-Segment Account of Tool-Selection Failures in LLM Agents
summary: LLM agents mis-call tools, and the natural guess is that the model failed to see the right tool in a crowded harness. We show the opposite through a lens concurrent work sets aside -- the model's attention to labeled tool-definition segments. On real BFCL failures, by per-candidate attention argmax the model attends most to the correct tool 80% of the time (vs. 21% chance), and the gold is the under-attended segment on only 10%: it looks at the right tool and still picks wrong. This directly refutes the intuitive "crowded-harness / lost-in-the-middle" explanation: the failure is at the decision readout, not the harness, and we pin it there three ways. (1) Input vs. readout: repairing the prompt (reordering or duplicating the gold tool) recovers <=23% of failures, while readout-side interventions recover 59-91%. (2) Representation-invariance: two gold-pointed interventions in different representations -- an additive attention-logit bias and a residual-stream steering vector -- recover largely the same failures (per-task Jaccard 0.865 pooled, 0.79-0.91 per model), so the bottleneck is localized to the readout independent of which representation is poked. (3) A training-free, gold-free selector: per-segment attention closes most of the gold-free-vs-oracle gap on BFCL (+11.9 pts pooled function-name selection vs. +17.9-pt oracle headroom) and adds +14.9 pts on Seal-Tools; every model positive (exact McNemar p<=8e-4 each). Scopes differ: the causal attention-bias dose-response is bidirectional and monotonic on 10 mask-honoring models (3-32B), the full 0.5-32B span carrying only the correlational diagnostic; the deployable selector is evaluated on 5 single-turn models and does not yet transfer to a multi-turn loop.

AI.tldr: 本文通过分析大语言模型（LLM）在工具调用失败时的注意力机制，发现模型通常能正确关注到正确工具定义，但仍在决策输出阶段出错；作者从输入vs.读出、表征不变性、无金标选择器三方面证实失败瓶颈在于读出层，并提出无需训练、无需金标的基于注意力的选择器，显著提升工具选择准确率。
AI.motivation: 直觉认为LLM工具调用失败是因为在众多工具中‘迷失于中间’（crowded-harness），即模型未能注意到正确工具；本文质疑该假设，转而聚焦模型对工具定义段的注意力分布，探究失败的真实根源。
AI.method: 基于BFCL真实失败案例，量化模型在各工具定义段上的注意力（per-candidate attention argmax）；设计三类实验验证失败定位：(1)对比prompt修改（输入侧）与readout干预（如attention bias、residual steering）的效果；(2)检验不同表征空间下干预的失败修复重合度（Jaccard相似性）；(3)构建仅依赖每段注意力分数的gold-free selector。
AI.result: 模型在80%失败案例中注意力最集中于正确工具（远超随机21%）；仅10%失败源于未关注正确工具；readout干预修复59–91%失败，远优于prompt修复（≤23%）；两种readout干预在不同表征下修复高度一致（Jaccard 0.79–0.91）；gold-free selector在BFCL上提升+11.9分（达oracle差距的66%），Seal-Tools上+14.9分，所有模型显著提升（p≤8e-4）。
AI.conclusion: LLM工具调用失败主因不在工具检索（harness）阶段，而在决策读出（readout）阶段；该瓶颈具有表征不变性；基于注意力的轻量级读出修正策略可高效、泛化地缓解问题，为部署级工具调用优化提供新路径。

title: Bayesian Networks with Latent Time Embedding for Stage-Aware Causal Modeling of Alzheimer's Disease Progression
summary: Alzheimer's disease (AD) progression is often described through the amyloid-tau-neurodegeneration, or AT(N), cascade. However, most longitudinal models represent this cascade either as a fixed sequence of biomarkers or as a black-box forecasting task. This makes it difficult to determine when biologically guided biomarker relationships influence future regional pathology. In this study, we introduce Bayesian Networks with Latent Time Embedding (BN-LTE), a Bayesian structural framework for stage-aware modeling of AD progression. BN-LTE estimates disease pseudotime from baseline biomarker profiles and constrains directed dependencies according to biologically plausible AT(N) ordering. Posterior spline-varying structural equations are then used to link initial multimodal measurements with future annualized regional tau-PET change. Across repeated subject-disjoint evaluations using ADNI data, BN-LTE shows strong spatial reconstruction of tau progression compared with the included forecasting baselines. Beyond spatial reconstruction, BN-LTE recovers posterior stage-varying AT(N)-constrained effects and identifies a mid-pseudotime window of amyloid sensitivity. This window is supported by model-implied g-formula contrasts, root-adjusted AIPW, mechanism-sensitive ablations, and robustness analyses across spline and prior specifications. Overall, these findings position BN-LTE as a Bayesian structural framework for forecasting tau progression while examining stage-dependent AT(N)-cascade mechanisms in observational longitudinal neuroimaging data. Our code is available at https://github.com/danleneurocom/BN-LTE.

AI.tldr: 本文提出BN-LTE方法，结合贝叶斯网络与潜在时间嵌入，实现对阿尔茨海默病（AD）中tau蛋白异常进展的阶段感知建模，并揭示了淀粉样蛋白敏感性的关键伪时间窗口。
AI.motivation: 现有AD纵向模型多为固定序列或黑箱预测，难以刻画生物机制驱动的生物标志物时序关系如何影响未来区域性病理变化。
AI.method: 提出贝叶斯网络与潜在时间嵌入（BN-LTE），从基线生物标志物估计疾病伪时间，按AT(N)生物学顺序约束有向依赖，并采用后验样条变结构方程建模基线多模态数据与未来年度区域性tau-PET变化的关系。
AI.result: 在ADNI数据上多次被试不相交评估中，BN-LTE显著优于基线方法的空间tau进展重建能力；并识别出一个中期伪时间窗口的淀粉样蛋白敏感性，该发现经g-formula对比、AIPW校正、机制敏感消融及多种样条/先验鲁棒性检验支持。
AI.conclusion: BN-LTE是一种可解释的贝叶斯结构框架，既能预测tau进展，又能检验观察性纵向神经影像数据中阶段依赖的AT(N)级联机制。

title: Binary Tracking for Spatial QA and Navigation with Open Vision-Language Models
comment: 21 pages, 4 figures, 15 tables. Project page: https://ndb796.github.io/BinaryTracking ; Code and dataset: https://github.com/ndb796/BinaryTracking
summary: This work addresses spatial question answering for service robots traversing long egocentric routes. Given a query such as "where can I find a dry cleaner on the way back home?", the system returns a metric coordinate that downstream navigation components can act on. Prior Spatial Question Answering approaches leverage retrieval-augmented agents built on closed-source models such as GPT-4o for path exploration. However, robots operating in the real world often cannot reliably depend on online closed-source models due to network instability, communication latency, and deployment cost. It creates a need for open-source based Spatial Question Answering approaches that can run onboard the robot, yet prior research in this direction remains limited. This work proposes BinTrack, a simple yet effective, fully open-source spatial-localization agent that leverages the temporal ordering of a robot's trajectory. BinTrack performs a binary search over the trajectory segments between two anchor landmarks identified from a query. It improves overall accuracy by up to 22.8% over other open-source implementations and even matches the reported closed-source model result on the global category of the SpaceLocQA benchmark, the most challenging setting that has so far required strong reasoning agents such as GPT-4o. Furthermore, its optimized inference strategy consistently yields more than a 1.5x inference speedup over previous approaches. Finally, this work releases GangnamLoop, a novel and practical multi-trip outdoor benchmark collected by deploying a real quadruped robot on public streets with the anonymization policy. It revisits the same locations under different outdoor conditions and pairs the robot's low viewpoint with the human owner's. The source codes and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/ndb796/BinaryTracking

AI.tldr: 本文提出BinTrack，一种完全开源的、基于机器人轨迹时间顺序的空间定位代理，用于服务机器人在长距离自视角路径中进行空间问答，显著提升了准确性和推理速度，并发布了新的多行程户外基准数据集GangnamLoop。
AI.motivation: 现实世界中的机器人难以依赖在线闭源模型（如GPT-4o）进行空间问答，因其受网络不稳、通信延迟和部署成本限制，亟需可在机器人本体运行的开源方案。
AI.method: BinTrack利用查询中识别出的两个锚点地标，在轨迹段上执行二分搜索，结合机器人轨迹的时间序信息实现空间定位；采用优化的推理策略提升效率。
AI.result: 在SpaceLocQA基准的全局类别上达到与GPT-4o相当的性能，准确率较其他开源方法最高提升22.8%，推理速度提升1.5倍以上；并发布新基准GangnamLoop及配套开源代码与数据集。
AI.conclusion: BinTrack证明了轻量、开源、本体化空间问答系统的可行性与高效性，为真实场景下服务机器人提供了实用且鲁棒的解决方案。

title: CoMNeT: A MedNeXt-CorrDiff Framework for Volumetric Brain Tumor Segmentation
summary: Accurate brain tumor segmentation from multiparametric magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is critical for treatment planning, response assessment, and quantitative neuro-oncology research. However, automated segmentation remains a difficult task in computer vision because of variation in tumor appearance and MRI protocols across patient scans. Moreover, clinically important regions such as enhancing tumor (ET) and tumor core (TC) are often small relative to the full brain volume, furthering increasing the difficulty of achieving high voxel-level precision. In this paper, we show that combining a modern 3D convolutional segmentation model with corrective diffusion-based refinement and ensembling improves volumetric glioma segmentation on the UTSW-Glioma dataset. We propose CoMNeT, a MedNeXt-CorrDiff framework that uses four MRI modalities as input and predicts ET, TC, and whole tumor (WT) regions for automated brain tumor segmentation. MedNeXt is used as the primary segmentation model with Global Response Normalization for feature learning, while CorrDiff is trained as a postprocessing residual refinement method to correct errors in the probability maps before final thresholding. Using five-fold cross-validation, CoMNeT achieved the highest Dice score for most tumor regions, with ET, TC, WT, and average Dice scores of 0.7543 +/- 0.0261, 0.6806 +/- 0.0166, 0.9049 +/- 0.0128, and 0.7798 +/- 0.0184, respectively. CoMNeT outperformed two selected baseline models: SegResNet (0.7555 +/- 0.0190 average Dice) and standalone MedNeXt (0.7697 +/- 0.0154 average Dice). Our findings support the use of corrective diffusion and fold-level probability ensembling as practical additions to existing state-of-the-art 3D convolutional models for automated glioma segmentation.

AI.tldr: 本文提出CoMNeT框架，结合MedNeXt主分割模型与CorrDiff扩散校正后处理方法，在UTSW-Glioma数据集上实现了更精准的胶质瘤多区域（ET、TC、WT）三维MRI自动分割。
AI.motivation: 脑肿瘤在MRI中形态和扫描协议差异大，且关键子区域（如ET、TC）体积小，导致自动化分割精度低。
AI.method: 提出CoMNeT框架：以MedNeXt（含全局响应归一化）为主干网络进行初始分割；引入CorrDiff作为基于扩散的残差校正模块优化概率图；结合五折交叉验证与概率图集成策略。
AI.result: 在UTSW-Glioma数据集上，ET、TC、WT和平均Dice分数分别为0.7543±0.0261、0.6806±0.0166、0.9049±0.0128和0.7798±0.0184，优于SegResNet和单独MedNeXt。
AI.conclusion: 校正性扩散建模与折叠级概率集成可有效提升现有3D卷积模型在胶质瘤分割任务中的性能，具备临床落地潜力。

title: PaperJury: Due-Process Review for Bounded LaTeX Revision
summary: Pre-submission hardening of human-authored LaTeX computer science papers differs from drafting assistance because it requires adversarial whole-paper review, explicit no-fix outcomes, and bounded artifact-safe revision. Existing writing assistants, critique generators, and judge-centered loops lack durable issue identity across rounds, deterministic routing from critique to adjudication, and manuscript control that can reject invalid concerns or defer author-dependent ones. We present PaperJury, a closed-loop review-verdict-revise-verify system built on a deterministic-versus-semantic split: deterministic orchestration manages decomposition, a frozen claim spine, a durable ledger, routing, stopping, and exact-once patch application, while semantic agents are limited to bounded review, judgment, and repair. PaperJury combines bounded holistic review, contestability-based routing, a due-process trial, and risk-proportional guard chains for anchor-bounded edits, yielding terminal outcomes of invalid-drop, valid-fixable, and author-required. In a two-arm expert-review evaluation on held-out Vision, natural language processing, and machine learning papers against four baselines, we assess issue quality, verdict and routing quality, edit safety, convergence behavior, and cost, supporting the thesis that load-bearing safety and completion logic should reside in deterministic orchestration rather than model discretion. PaperJury is available at https://github.com/u7079256/paperjury.

AI.tldr: 本文提出PaperJury系统，通过确定性编排与语义代理分离的架构，实现对LaTeX论文的预提交加固，确保问题可追溯、路由可判定、修改安全可控，并在多领域论文评估中验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有写作辅助工具缺乏跨轮次的问题身份持久性、从批评到裁决的确定性路由，以及能拒绝无效关切或延迟作者依赖型问题的手稿控制能力；而预提交加固需对抗性全文审查、明确不修复结果和受限的安全修订。
AI.method: 提出PaperJury——一个闭环的“评审-裁决-修订-验证”系统，采用确定性（管理分解、冻结主张主干、持久账本、路由、终止、精确一次补丁应用）与语义（受限于审查、判断、修复）分离架构，并引入基于可争议性的路由、正当程序式审理及风险适配的锚定编辑防护链。
AI.result: 在视觉、自然语言处理和机器学习领域的论文上对比四类基线，评估显示PaperJury在问题质量、裁决与路由质量、编辑安全性、收敛行为和成本方面表现优异，证实负载关键的安全与完成逻辑应置于确定性编排而非模型自主决策中。
AI.conclusion: PaperJury证明了将安全性和完成逻辑交由确定性编排而非大模型自主判断，可更可靠地实现论文预提交加固；其开源实现已在GitHub发布。

title: Posterior Twins: Distributional Behavioral Simulation for Enterprise Decisions
summary: Enterprise behavioral simulation requires more than producing a plausible response. Many decisions depend on the shape of a population under a proposed action: which segments accept, defect, hesitate, or move into risk-sensitive states. This paper introduces Posterior Twins, a memory-grounded digital-twin approach that represents likely behavior as an updated distribution under a specific decision context. We evaluate a family of Twinning Labs behavioral-model operating points on a 226-example held-out behavioral-response benchmark and report both modal accuracy and Wasserstein-1 distance. The results show that modal accuracy and distributional fidelity identify different operating regimes. TL-Twin Alpha achieves the lowest observed Wasserstein-1 distance in the reported result set ($W_1 = 1.16$), while TL-Twin Delta and TL-Twin Gamma provide balanced operating points near the modal-accuracy frontier. The paper frames these results as a systems result: governed memory, behavioral model routing, scenario orchestration, distributional aggregation, and auditability are necessary for turning simulated behavior into reusable enterprise decision evidence.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为'后验孪生（Posterior Twins）'的数字孪生方法，用于企业行为模拟，强调在特定决策背景下对行为分布的更新建模，并通过Wasserstein-1距离和模态准确率评估不同模型变体的性能，指出分布保真度与点估计准确率反映不同运行模式，同时强调系统级能力（如受控记忆、模型路由、场景编排等）对生成可审计、可复用的企业决策证据至关重要。
AI.motivation: 企业行为模拟不仅需生成合理响应，更需刻画群体在特定决策下的行为分布（如接受、流失、犹豫、风险敏感等细分状态），以支撑关键决策。
AI.method: 提出'后验孪生'方法——一种基于记忆的数字孪生框架，将行为建模为在具体决策上下文下更新的概率分布；在226例留出测试集上评估多个Twinning Labs（TL-Twin）行为模型变体，使用模态准确率和Wasserstein-1距离双重指标。
AI.result: TL-Twin Alpha取得最低Wasserstein-1距离（W₁ = 1.16），体现最优分布保真度；TL-Twin Delta与Gamma则在模态准确率前沿附近提供平衡表现；两类指标揭示了不同的模型运行机制。
AI.conclusion: 单纯优化点估计（如模态准确率）不足以满足企业决策需求；需构建包含受控记忆、行为模型路由、场景编排、分布聚合与可审计性在内的系统级能力，才能将模拟行为转化为可信、可复用的决策证据。

title: Proximal Policy Optimization for Amortized Discrete Sampling
summary: This paper explores policy gradient algorithms for training stochastic policies to sample from structured discrete probability distributions under the Generative Flow Network (GFlowNet) framework. Building on extensive theoretical connections between GFlowNets and entropy-regularized reinforcement learning, we derive equivalents of standard policy gradient algorithms for training GFlowNets, as well as experimentally explore their various methodological aspects, including baseline training and advantage estimation. Most importantly, our work is the first to derive and successfully apply proximal policy optimization to GFlowNets, showing its improved convergence speed and data efficiency compared to standard GFlowNet training objectives on benchmarks ranging from synthetic energies to molecular graph generation.

AI.tldr: 本文将策略梯度算法引入生成流网络（GFlowNet）框架，首次成功应用近端策略优化（PPO）训练GFlowNets，在合成能量和分子图生成等任务上展现出更快的收敛速度与更高的数据效率。
AI.motivation: 基于GFlowNets与熵正则化强化学习之间的深刻理论联系，探索如何利用成熟的策略梯度方法提升GFlowNet训练效果。
AI.method: 推导了标准策略梯度算法在GFlowNet中的对应形式，并实验研究了基线训练、优势估计等关键组件；首次将近端策略优化（PPO）适配并应用于GFlowNet训练。
AI.result: PPO显著提升了GFlowNet的收敛速度和数据效率，在合成能量建模和分子图生成等基准任务上优于传统GFlowNet训练目标。
AI.conclusion: 策略梯度方法，尤其是PPO，是训练GFlowNets的有效新范式，为结构化离散分布建模提供了更高效、更稳定的优化路径。

title: Unified Motion-Action Modeling for Heterogeneous Robot Learning
summary: We present Unified Motion-Action (UMA) Model, an approach that uses 3D object motion trajectories as a shared interface to bridge visuomotor control and dynamics modeling. UMA treats object motion and robot actions as co-evolving variables under a masked generative objective, in which the mask pattern determines both the supervision regime during pretraining and the inference mode at deployment. Using hindsight-relabeled motion contexts and a contrastive objective that disentangles task intent from scene geometry, UMA enables multi-task pretraining across heterogeneous data sources without requiring manually annotated task instructions. At deployment, the same pretrained parameters support motion-conditioned visuomotor control, motion-based dynamics modeling, and task adaptation from few-shot demonstrations. Pretrained on a mixture of robot demonstrations, human videos, and simulated data, UMA consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines specialized for each inference mode.

AI.tldr: UMA模型利用3D物体运动轨迹作为统一接口，联合建模机器人动作与物体动力学，通过掩码生成目标实现多任务预训练，并在部署时支持多种下游任务。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在 visuomotor 控制与动力学建模之间缺乏统一表征，且依赖大量人工标注的任务指令，难以跨异构数据源高效预训练。
AI.method: 提出Unified Motion-Action (UMA) 模型，将物体运动与机器人动作视为共演变量，在掩码生成框架下进行预训练；引入回溯重标记的运动上下文和解耦任务意图与场景几何的对比目标。
AI.result: UMA在机器人演示、人类视频与仿真数据混合预训练后，在运动条件下的 visuomotor 控制、基于运动的动力学建模及少样本任务适配等多任务上均超越各专用SOTA基线。
AI.conclusion: UMA验证了以3D运动轨迹为统一接口构建通用具身智能模型的可行性，实现了跨模态、跨任务、跨数据源的一体化建模。

title: Conditional Multi-Event Temporal Grounding in Long-Form Video
summary: Multimodal large language models have made rapid progress in video temporal grounding, yet real-world applications routinely require localizing every event that satisfies compositional temporal and spatial conditions. Existing benchmarks fall short: they localize only a single moment per query, count without temporal conditions, or treat grounding and counting as disjoint tasks. We introduce CoMET-Bench for Conditional Multi-Event Temporal Grounding in long-form video, comprising 2789 queries over 600 videos averaging 33.8 minutes across five real-world domains, with each query composed from 4 temporal conditions, 3 spatial conditions, and a dedicated negative-query subset. We further propose a unified evaluation protocol jointly measuring counting, grounding, and negative-query recognition, including a new Rejection-F1 metric that prevents trivial gaming by lazy "always-empty" models. Benchmarking a broad suite of MLLMs, agent-based, and grounding-specialized methods reveals that existing approaches remain far from solving this task. Building on these findings, we propose CoMET-Agent, a training-free agentic framework that reformulates the task as structured search-and-aggregate, improving F1@0.5 by 6.1% over GPT-5 purely through structural reasoning. Failure analysis further surfaces three open directions: fine-grained entity tracking, position-uniform retrieval, and causal event pairing.

AI.tldr: 本文提出CoMET-Bench基准和CoMET-Agent框架，用于解决长视频中满足复合时空条件的多事件时序定位问题，推动多模态大模型在真实场景下的细粒度视频理解能力。
AI.motivation: 现有视频时序定位基准仅支持单事件定位、缺乏复合时空条件建模能力，且未统一评估定位、计数与负查询识别，难以支撑真实场景中复杂事件检索需求。
AI.method: 构建CoMET-Bench基准（含2789个复合时空条件查询、600个长视频），设计联合评估协议（含新指标Rejection-F1）；提出无需训练的CoMET-Agent框架，将任务建模为结构化搜索与聚合。
AI.result: 在CoMET-Bench上全面评测MLLMs、Agent方法及专用定位模型，发现当前方法性能仍远未达标；CoMET-Agent相较GPT-5提升F1@0.5达6.1%。
AI.conclusion: 复合多事件时序定位是亟待突破的新挑战；CoMET-Bench与CoMET-Agent为该方向提供了基准、评估标准与可行范式，揭示了实体细粒度追踪、位置无偏检索和因果事件配对三大开放问题。

title: TMASC: Transmasculine Attitude and Speech Corpus
summary: We introduce the Transmasculine Attitudes and Speech Corpus (TMASC), a multimodal corpus of 196 transmasculine individuals, including questionnaire responses and 66 audio recordings. The questionnaire includes items exploring the vocal health of transmasculine individuals. The audio recordings include cough and throat-clearing samples, a reading passage, and additional session-specific questions. This paper outlines the development of this corpus and the data collection procedures. To illustrate the utility of this corpus, we present three case studies demonstrating how this crowd-sourced multimodal corpus can be used to support transmasculine individuals. These include the integration of perceptual and acoustic data, the identification of group-level characteristics, and the calibration of acoustic measurements.

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了TMASC语料库，一个包含196名跨性别男性个体的多模态语料库，包括问卷调查和66段音频录音，并通过三个案例研究展示了其应用价值。
AI.motivation: 为支持跨性别男性个体，构建一个包含语音健康信息和多模态数据的专用语料库。
AI.method: 开发了Transmasculine Attitudes and Speech Corpus（TMASC），收集问卷数据与音频样本（咳嗽、清嗓、朗读及定制问题），并开展三项案例研究验证其效用。
AI.result: 成功构建TMASC语料库，并通过案例研究展示了其在感知与声学数据整合、群体特征识别及声学测量校准方面的应用潜力。
AI.conclusion: TMASC是一个有价值的开源多模态资源，可推动跨性别男性语音健康研究与临床支持发展。

title: When Agent Automation Becomes Profitable: Quantifying and Insuring Autonomous AI Risk through Trace-Economic Underwriting
summary: AI agents can now take irreversible actions in operational systems, but agent-caused losses are still not clearly assigned, priced, or transferred. Providers often disclaim consequential damages, users are left with uncompensated losses, and default human review limits the efficiency gains of automation. We ask when autonomous AI deployment can become economically acceptable despite failure risk. Our answer is to quantify risk at the customer-task-trace episode level and transfer it through insurance. Automation is acceptable when its expected benefit exceeds the premium, control cost, and remaining risk. This requires a defined role with bounded permissions and comparable traces. We introduce trace-economic underwriting, which maps tool-use traces to customer exposure and claimable loss, then uses this representation for pricing, control, and risk transfer. It uses deterministic economic labels rather than an LLM judge. In our trace-to-loss testbed, trace-economic pricing reduces pricing MAE from $17.7K to $569 and removes regressive cross-subsidy. A 300-trace expert audit accepts 295 labels unchanged. On 1,000 real SWE-smith traces, trace-conditioned controls reduce CVaR95 by 72%. Theorem~1 gives a finite-sample scope condition. We release code, labels, and audit sheets.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为‘trace-economic underwriting’的风险量化与转移框架，通过将AI代理的操作轨迹映射到客户暴露和可索赔损失，实现对自主AI部署风险的精确定价、控制与保险转移，显著提升自动化经济可行性。
AI.motivation: AI代理已在运营系统中执行不可逆操作，但其引发的损失缺乏明确的责任归属、定价与风险转移机制，导致用户承担未补偿损失，且人工审核制约自动化效率。
AI.method: 提出‘trace-economic underwriting’方法：在客户-任务-轨迹层面量化风险；用确定性经济标签（而非LLM评判）映射工具调用轨迹至损失；结合保费、控制成本与剩余风险建模经济可接受性；引入定理1给出有限样本适用范围条件。
AI.result: 在测试平台中，轨迹-经济定价将平均绝对误差从17.7K降至569美元，并消除累退税式交叉补贴；300轨迹专家审计中295个标签获认可；在1000条真实SWE-smith轨迹上，轨迹条件化控制使CVaR95降低72%。
AI.conclusion: 当预期收益超过保费、控制成本与剩余风险时，自主AI部署具备经济可行性；该框架通过明确定义角色、权限边界与可比轨迹，实现了AI风险的可度量、可定价、可保险，为AI规模化落地提供经济基础设施。

title: Mean-Field Parallel Decoding for Discrete Diffusion Language Models
summary: Discrete diffusion language models enable parallel token generation, offering a pathway to low-latency decoding. However, selecting tokens independently by marginal confidence limits effective parallelism: tokens that appear reliable in isolation can form incompatible configurations when several positions are updated at once. We introduce a training-free decoding framework that coordinates these parallel updates. At each forward pass, the method assigns a commit score to each masked position and refines these scores using pairwise interactions derived from the model's predictive distributions. A variational relaxation yields a simple fixed-point update that suppresses conflicting simultaneous commitments within a single forward pass. This mechanism allows the decoder to commit more tokens in parallel while maintaining competitive generation quality. The method is lightweight, requires no auxiliary model or retraining, and drops into existing diffusion decoding pipelines without modification. Experiments on reasoning and code-generation benchmarks show consistent improvements in the quality-latency trade-off.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需训练的离散扩散语言模型解码框架，通过引入基于预测分布的成对交互和变分松弛的固定点更新机制，在不牺牲生成质量的前提下提升并行token生成能力，从而改善质量-延迟权衡。
AI.motivation: 离散扩散语言模型虽支持并行token生成以降低延迟，但独立按边缘置信度选token会导致多位置同时更新时出现不兼容配置，限制了实际并行效率。
AI.method: 提出一种训练无关的解码框架：在每次前向传播中为每个掩码位置分配commit score，并利用模型预测分布导出的成对交互关系进行score精炼；通过变分松弛得到简单固定点更新规则，抑制单次前向中冲突的并行提交。
AI.result: 该方法显著提升了并行提交token数量，同时保持竞争力的生成质量；在推理与代码生成基准上一致改善质量-延迟权衡；无需额外模型或重训练，可即插即用到现有扩散解码流程中。
AI.conclusion: 该解码机制是一种轻量、通用且高效的改进方案，有效缓解离散扩散模型中并行解码的冲突问题，为低延迟高质量文本生成提供了新思路。

title: CrossMaps: Confidence-Aware Open-Vocabulary Semantic Mapping for Rover Navigation
comment: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2026: ROSE International Workshop on Robotics Software Engineering, June 01, 2026, Vienna, Austria
summary: Rovers rely on perception to maintain spatial maps that encode both objects and sensor quality (e.g., range reliability, lighting artifacts, data density), guiding data fusion, embedding updates, and navigation under partial observability. To study these coupled perception-navigation processes, we present CrossMaps, a real-time confidence-aware open-vocabulary semantic mapping pipeline that constructs language-queryable maps from RGB-D data. Building on VLMaps-style approaches, CrossMaps integrates multi-scale CLIP embeddings with confidence-aware fusion and a dual-memory architecture consisting of Short-Term Memory (STM) and Long-Term Memory (LTM). The STM aggregates noisy visual observations using geometric, semantic, and temporal confidence cues, while confident and coherent cells are promoted to the LTM as persistent semantic landmarks. Designed for deployment with a Jetson Orin-powered UGV alongside SLAM, CrossMaps runs in real time and produces semantic heatmaps that can be queried with natural language to guide rover navigation.

AI.tldr: CrossMaps是一种实时、置信度感知的开放词汇语义建图方法，融合多尺度CLIP嵌入与双记忆架构（STM/LTM），支持自然语言查询，适用于资源受限的UGV平台。
AI.motivation: 解决火星/野外探测中感知与导航耦合问题，需在部分可观测条件下同时建模物体语义与传感器质量（如测距可靠性、光照伪影、数据密度）。
AI.method: 基于VLMaps框架，引入多尺度CLIP视觉-语言嵌入；设计置信度感知融合机制，结合几何、语义和时间置信线索；构建短时记忆（STM）用于聚合噪声观测，长时记忆（LTM）存储高置信语义地标；与SLAM协同部署于Jetson Orin平台。
AI.result: 实现实时运行，在Jetson Orin驱动的UGV上生成可自然语言查询的语义热力图，提升部分可观测环境下的导航鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: CrossMaps验证了将传感器置信度建模与开放词汇语义映射统一建模的有效性，为自主 rover 在复杂野外环境中的语义感知与决策提供了新范式。

title: PPDM: Pixel Puzzling Diffusion Model for Speed and Memory Efficient Volumetric Medical Image Translation
summary: Diffusion models have demonstrated superior fidelity for medical image-to-image translation, but their extension to high-resolution 3D volumes is severely constrained by prohibitive computational cost and GPU memory requirements. Existing memory-efficient strategies often compromise global volumetric consistency or fine anatomical detail. In this work, we propose the Pixel Puzzling Diffusion Model (PPDM), a simple and effective framework for memory- and speed-efficient 3D medical image translation. PPDM introduces a reversible pixel puzzle-unpuzzle operator that trades spatial resolution for channel dimensionality, substantially reducing activation memory while preserving global context. To further improve efficiency and stability, we adopt a direct bridge diffusion formulation that starts from the conditional input rather than pure noise, enabling the model to focus on task-relevant residuals. In addition, a puzzle-gradient loss is incorporated to enforce spatial coherence and suppress grid-like artifacts introduced by spatial rearrangement. We evaluate PPDM on multiple challenging 3D medical image translation tasks, including low-count PET denoising, joint PET denoising and attenuation correction, and cross-modal MRI translation. Across all tasks, PPDM consistently matches or outperforms full 3D diffusion models while reducing training GPU memory usage by up to an order of magnitude and significantly accelerating inference, and it outperforms existing memory-efficient diffusion approaches based on latent compression or frequency decomposition. These results demonstrate that PPDM provides a practical and scalable solution for high-fidelity 3D diffusion-based medical image translation under limited computational resources.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Pixel Puzzling Diffusion Model（PPDM）的新框架，用于高效、低内存开销的3D医学图像翻译。其核心是可逆的像素拼图操作、直接桥接扩散建模和拼图梯度损失，显著降低GPU内存需求并保持全局一致性和细节质量。
AI.motivation: 扩散模型在3D高分辨率医学图像翻译中受限于高昂计算与显存开销，现有轻量方法常牺牲全局一致性或解剖细节。
AI.method: 提出PPDM框架：1）可逆像素拼图-解拼图操作，将空间分辨率转为通道维度以节省显存；2）采用从条件输入出发的直接桥接扩散；3）引入拼图梯度损失抑制网格伪影并增强空间连贯性。
AI.result: 在低计数PET去噪、PET联合去噪与衰减校正、跨模态MRI翻译等任务上，PPDM性能媲美或超越全3D扩散模型，显存降低达一个数量级，推理显著加速，并优于基于潜在压缩或频域分解的现有轻量方法。
AI.conclusion: PPDM为资源受限场景下实现高保真3D扩散医学图像翻译提供了实用且可扩展的解决方案。

title: Tyler: Typed Latent Reasoning for Language Models -- When to Think, What to Compute, and How Much to Allocate
summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting improves reasoning in large language models (LLMs) by externalizing intermediate computation as discrete text tokens, but this textual interface also introduces redundancy and inference overhead. Latent reasoning offers a promising alternative by carrying part of the computation in continuous representations. However, existing methods typically predefine when latent computation is invoked and how it is allocated during decoding, leaving a key problem unresolved: when to invoke latent computation, what type of computation to perform, and how much budget to allocate. We propose \textbf{Ty}ped \textbf{L}at\textbf{e}nt \textbf{R}easoning (Tyler), a typed and budget-aware framework for latent reasoning during autoregressive decoding. Tyler learns a policy that, at each decoding step, chooses between emitting a text token and switching to a latent computation module specialized for a particular reasoning function. Once invoked, an operator maps the current reasoning state into latent tokens that support global planning, local state updates, or reusable procedural abstraction. Across extensive experiments on three backbone LLMs, Tyler improves accuracy by up to 14.49 points over CoT and by up to 4.30 points over the strongest competing baseline. It further generalizes across diverse reasoning domains and achieves the best final-stage performance with the lowest forgetting.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Tyler框架，一种类型化且预算感知的潜在推理方法，在自回归解码过程中动态决定何时、以何种类型及多少计算资源调用潜在推理模块，显著提升大语言模型的推理准确率与泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有链式思维（CoT）提示存在文本冗余和推理开销问题；而现有潜在推理方法预设调用时机与计算分配方式，未能解决‘何时调用、调用何种类型、分配多少预算’这一关键问题。
AI.method: Tyler是一种类型化、预算感知的潜在推理框架：在每步解码中，学习一个策略来选择输出文本token或切换至特定推理功能的潜在计算模块；被调用的算子将当前推理状态映射为支持全局规划、局部状态更新或可复用过程抽象的潜在token。
AI.result: 在三个主流大语言模型上，Tyler相较链式思维提升最高达14.49分准确率，相较最强基线提升最高达4.30分；具备跨推理领域的泛化能力，并在最终阶段性能最优且遗忘最少。
AI.conclusion: Tyler通过动态、类型化、预算感知的潜在推理机制，有效弥补了显式文本推理与静态潜在推理之间的鸿沟，为高效、灵活的大模型推理提供了新范式。

title: Tensor-Coord: Algebraic Decomposition of Joint Plan Tensors for Conflict-Free Multi-Agent LLM Planning
summary: Large language models (LLMs) remain limited in multi-agent planning because independently generated plans can create coordination failures such as spatial collisions, resource contention, and temporal deadlocks. We introduce Tensor-Coord, a multilinear algebra framework that represents the joint plan of N agents as a third-order tensor \(T \in R^{N \times H \times A}\) over agents, timesteps, and actions. Canonical Polyadic (CP) and Tucker decompositions are used to identify latent coordination structure. The minimal epsilon-approximate CP rank R* defines a computable coordination complexity measure, with \(CC(Pi)=(R*-N)/N\). We prove that R*=N is necessary and sufficient for plan independence. The residual \(E=T-T_{R*}\) defines a conflict score over agent pairs, timesteps, and actions, localizing failures without domain-specific rules. Tucker factors provide interpretable agent roles, temporal phases, and action clusters that are converted into natural language constraints for iterative LLM replanning. Experiments on multi-robot delivery tasks across Easy (2 agents, 5x5 grid), Medium (3 agents, 5x5 grid), and Hard (4 agents, 5x5 grid) settings show convergence to conflict-free plans in 100% of 2-agent cases within 1.4 iterations on average, 80% of 3-agent cases within 3.2 iterations, and 60% of 4-agent cases within 4.0 iterations. CP rank scaled approximately linearly as \(R*(N) = 3.9N + 0.5\), supporting its use as a predictor of coordination complexity.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Tensor-Coord框架，利用张量分解建模多智能体联合计划，量化协调复杂度，并定位冲突，指导LLM迭代重规划，显著提升多机器人任务中的协调成功率。
AI.motivation: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在多智能体规划中受限于独立生成计划导致的空间碰撞、资源争用和时间死锁等协调失败问题。
AI.method: 提出Tensor-Coord框架：将N个智能体的联合计划表示为三阶张量T∈R^(N×H×A)，采用CP与Tucker分解挖掘潜在协调结构；定义最小ε近似CP秩R*作为可计算的协调复杂度指标CC(Π)=(R*−N)/N；利用残差E=T−T_R*生成细粒度冲突得分；Tucker因子提取可解释的角色、时相与动作簇，并转为自然语言约束用于LLM迭代重规划。
AI.result: 在多机器人配送任务实验中：2智能体（Easy）100%成功、平均1.4轮收敛；3智能体（Medium）80%成功、平均3.2轮；4智能体（Hard）60%成功、平均4.0轮；CP秩R*(N)≈3.9N+0.5，验证其作为协调复杂度预测指标的有效性。
AI.conclusion: Tensor-Coord提供了一种通用、可解释、可计算的多智能体协调建模与优化方法，无需领域规则即可定位冲突并引导LLM重规划，为LLM驱动的多智能体系统提供了新范式。

title: Continuous Cross-Domain Traffic State Prediction via Memory-Augmented Graph Liquid Time-Constant Networks
summary: Traffic state prediction is a fundamental task in intelligent transportation systems. In practical applications, some regions suffer from limited traffic observations due to insufficient sensing infrastructure, making cross-domain knowledge transfer an important solution for data-scarce traffic prediction. However, existing cross-domain traffic prediction methods still face several limitations, including coarse-grained source-target adaptation, limited capability in handling unseen target-domain patterns, and insufficient modeling of continuous traffic dynamics under irregular or heterogeneous temporal conditions. To address these issues, this paper proposes a continuous cross-domain traffic prediction framework, termed Memory-Augmented Graph Liquid Time-Constant Network (MA-GLTC). Specifically, we first construct spatio-temporal units (STUs) to decompose traffic networks into transferable local units, enabling fine-grained knowledge alignment across domains. Then, a graph liquid time-constant network (GLTC) is developed to model graph-coupled traffic evolution in continuous time. Different from generic graph neural ODE-based models, GLTC introduces graph-coupled recurrent conductance into liquid time-constant dynamics, allowing node states to evolve with leakage, adaptive time constants, and neighborhood-aware feedback. Furthermore, a Memory-based Transfer Storage (MTS) mechanism is designed to preserve source-domain knowledge, retrieve matched traffic patterns, and update reliable target-domain patterns when unseen states emerge. Experiments on five public traffic datasets demonstrate that MA-GLTC consistently outperforms representative innerdomain and cross-domain baselines in both short-term and longterm prediction tasks. Compared with the second-best method, MA-GLTC reduces the average prediction errors by 3.02%, 0.33%, 8.92%, 10.09%, and 2.11%, respectively.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种连续跨域交通状态预测框架MA-GLTC，通过构建时空单元（STUs）、图液态时间常数网络（GLTC）和基于记忆的迁移存储（MTS）机制，实现细粒度知识对齐、连续时间动态建模与源域知识有效迁移，在多个数据集上显著优于现有方法。
AI.motivation: 解决交通预测中数据稀缺区域的跨域知识迁移问题，克服现有方法在源-目标域适配粗糙、难以处理未见目标模式、以及不适应非规则/异构时间条件下的连续交通动态建模等局限。
AI.method: 提出Memory-Augmented Graph Liquid Time-Constant Network（MA-GLTC）：1）构建时空单元（STUs）实现细粒度跨域知识对齐；2）设计图液态时间常数网络（GLTC），引入图耦合递归电导机制，支持带泄漏、自适应时间常数和邻域感知反馈的连续时间节点状态演化；3）设计基于记忆的迁移存储（MTS）机制，用于保存源域知识、匹配检索交通模式，并在出现未见状态时更新可靠的目标域模式。
AI.result: 在五个公开交通数据集上的实验表明，MA-GLTC在短时与长时预测任务中均持续优于典型域内及跨域基线方法；相比次优方法，平均预测误差分别降低3.02%、0.33%、8.92%、10.09%和2.11%。
AI.conclusion: MA-GLTC通过细粒度结构分解、连续时间图动力学建模与记忆增强迁移机制，有效提升了跨域交通预测的泛化性与鲁棒性，为数据稀缺场景下的智能交通系统提供了新思路。

title: SidewalkBench: Benchmarking Visual Navigation on Urban Sidewalks
summary: Urban sidewalk navigation presents significant challenges due to complex structural layouts, dynamic pedestrian behaviors, and long distances. While recent visual navigation models offer a promising solution, the lack of a unified benchmark hinders quantitative and reproducible evaluation. To bridge this gap, we propose SidewalkBench, a comprehensive benchmark designed for visual navigation on urban sidewalks. Built upon NVIDIA Isaac Sim, SidewalkBench brings GPU-accelerated simulation of diverse, high-fidelity sidewalk environments, including both procedurally generated and real-world scanned scenes. We further populate the scenes with rich, reactive event-based pedestrian behaviors and flexible, efficient animation, enabling standardized model evaluation under realistic real-world settings. We conduct a comprehensive evaluation of 9 visual navigation models on 330 unit-test scenarios, 800 pedestrian-reactive scenarios, and 105 long-horizon scenarios. Our findings highlight that pedestrian interaction and long-horizon robustness remain critical bottlenecks for existing models, and scaling up sidewalk training with synthetic data emerges as a promising solution.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了SidewalkBench，一个基于NVIDIA Isaac Sim构建的高保真城市人行道视觉导航基准，涵盖程序生成与真实扫描场景、动态行人行为模拟，并在330个单元测试、800个行人响应及105个长程场景中评估了9种模型，揭示出行人交互与长程鲁棒性是当前瓶颈，合成数据扩增训练具潜力。
AI.motivation: 缺乏统一基准阻碍了城市人行道视觉导航模型的定量与可复现评估。
AI.method: 提出SidewalkBench基准，基于NVIDIA Isaac Sim构建多样化高保真仿真环境（含程序生成与真实扫描场景），集成事件驱动的动态行人行为与高效动画，并在三类共1235个场景中系统评估9种视觉导航模型。
AI.result: 评估发现行人交互能力和长时程鲁棒性是现有模型的关键瓶颈；利用合成数据扩大训练规模展现出提升性能的潜力。
AI.conclusion: SidewalkBench为城市人行道视觉导航提供了首个全面、标准化的评估平台，推动该领域向更真实、更具挑战性的场景发展。

title: SGFormer++: Semantic Graph Transformer for Incremental 3D Scene Graph Generation
summary: In this paper, we propose SGFormer++, a novel Semantic Graph Transformer for 3D scene graph generation (SGG), which aims to parse point cloud scenes into semantic structural graphs, where nodes denote detected object instances and edges encode their pairwise relationships, with the core challenge lying in modeling complex global scene structure. While existing graph convolutional network (GCN)-based methods suffer from over-smoothing and limited receptive fields, SGFormer++ leverages Transformer layers as its backbone to enable global message passing. Specifically, we introduce two key components tailored for 3D SGG: (1) a Graph Embedding Layer++ that efficiently integrates edge-aware global context with linear computational complexity, and (2) a Semantic Injection Layer++ that enriches visual features with linguistic priors from large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs), boosting semantic representation without introducing extra trainable parameters. To further address the practical challenge of incremental SGG (I-SGG), where new relationship categories arrive sequentially, we equip SGFormer++ with a novel Spatial-guided Feature Adapter, which calibrates predicate features using subject-object spatial geometry to counter scale variation, and a Cascaded Binary Prediction Head that mitigates catastrophic forgetting via task-incremental classifier expansion and logit distillation. Extensive experiments on the 3DSSG benchmark demonstrate that SGFormer++ achieves state-of-the-art performance in both standard and incremental settings: it yields a significant 4.49% absolute improvement in Predicate A@1 under the incremental setting. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/Andy20178/SGFormer.

AI.tldr: 本文提出SGFormer++，一种用于3D场景图生成（SGG）的语义图Transformer模型，通过引入图嵌入层++和语义注入层++提升全局建模与语义表征能力，并针对增量式SGG（I-SGG）设计空间引导特征适配器与级联二值预测头，显著提升性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于GCN的方法存在过平滑和感受野受限问题，且缺乏对复杂全局场景结构的有效建模；同时，增量式SGG中新增关系类别带来的灾难性遗忘与尺度变化挑战尚未被充分解决。
AI.method: 采用Transformer作为主干实现全局消息传递；设计图嵌入层++（线性复杂度、边感知全局上下文）和语义注入层++（融合LLM/VLM语言先验，无额外可训练参数）；为I-SGG引入空间引导特征适配器（利用主宾空间几何校准谓词特征）和级联二值预测头（任务增量分类器扩展+logit蒸馏）。
AI.result: 在3DSSG基准上，SGFormer++在标准和增量设置下均达到SOTA；增量设置下Predicate A@1绝对提升4.49%。
AI.conclusion: SGFormer++有效解决了3D SGG中全局结构建模与增量学习的关键挑战，验证了Transformer架构与多模态语义注入在该任务中的优越性。

title: Evaluating LLM Personalization via Semantic Constraint Verification
summary: Current evaluation paradigms for Large Language Model (LLM) personalization rely heavily on brittle surface-matching metrics or computationally expensive LLM-as-a-judge protocols, both of which lack interpretability. To address these limitations, we introduce Natural Language Inference Constraint Verification (NLICV), a scalable, semantically invariant framework that maps sentence meanings to truth-condition sets to verify personalization constraints via a Natural Language Inference (NLI) model. Moving beyond binary scoring, NLICV categorizes LLM behaviors into four distinct modes: personalization, generalization, sycophancy, and failure. Extensive experiments demonstrate that NLICV aligns closely with human annotations while drastically reducing the latency and token costs associated with LLM judges (up to 2100 inference speedup). Finally, through an ablation-based procedure, NLICV pinpoints the exact sentences driving the constraint verification, yielding faithful, understandable evidence for its evaluations.

AI.tldr: 本文提出NLICV框架，通过自然语言推理模型验证大语言模型个性化约束，具备可扩展性、语义不变性和高解释性，并显著降低计算开销。
AI.motivation: 现有大语言模型个性化评估范式依赖脆弱的表面匹配指标或计算昂贵的LLM-as-a-judge协议，缺乏可解释性。
AI.method: 提出自然语言推理约束验证（NLICV）框架，将句子语义映射为真值条件集，利用NLI模型验证个性化约束，并将LLM行为细分为四种模式。
AI.result: NLICV与人工标注高度一致，推理速度提升达2100倍，且能通过消融分析定位驱动评估的关键句子。
AI.conclusion: NLICV是一种高效、可解释、语义稳健的大语言模型个性化评估新范式。

title: Steering Emotional Dynamics for Art Therapy: Controllable Narrative Script Generation through Hierarchically Guided LLM Agents
summary: Art therapy plays a vital role in emotional healing, in which narrative creation acts as the primary vehicle for emotional expression. Given the inherently dynamic nature of emotions during healing, narratives with finely controlled emotional fluctuations enable individuals to safely project inner conflicts and achieve emotional catharsis. Recently, with the rapid development of Large Language Models (LLMs), automated narrative generation technology has provided a new pathway to support such artistic designs. However, while existing methods can produce fluent texts, they struggle to generate narratives that adhere to specified affective trajectories, failing to meet the demands of emotion-oriented psychological healing. To address these issues, this paper proposes EC-Script, an LLM agent-based framework that enables hierarchical control of the affective trajectory in narrative generation for emotional healing. To ensure that the generated narratives strictly follow the given emotional patterns, EC-Script establishes overall narrative direction through Emotion-Trajectory Planning, propels scene-level plot development with Character-Driven Scene Generation, and regulates local emotional changes of characters via Emotion-Controlled Script Writing. Ultimately, it outputs scene-by-scene script content that remains highly consistent with the preset affective trajectory. Experimental results demonstrate that EC-Script significantly outperforms baseline methods in affective trajectory adherence, exhibiting excellent and reliable emotional controllability, thereby providing effective technical support for AI-assisted emotional healing scenarios.

AI.tldr: 本文提出EC-Script框架，利用LLM代理实现叙事生成中情感轨迹的分层控制，以支持艺术治疗中的情绪疗愈。
AI.motivation: 现有大语言模型虽能生成流畅文本，但难以精准遵循预设情感轨迹，无法满足情绪导向心理疗愈的需求。
AI.method: EC-Script采用三层控制机制：情绪轨迹规划（整体方向）、角色驱动场景生成（场景级情节推进）、情绪可控剧本写作（角色局部情绪调节）。
AI.result: 实验表明EC-Script在情感轨迹一致性上显著优于基线方法，具备优异且稳定的情绪可控性。
AI.conclusion: EC-Script为AI辅助情绪疗愈提供了有效技术支撑，推动了叙事生成在艺术治疗中的实际应用。

title: Brownian Kernel Ladders
summary:
Constructing mathematically tractable function spaces that capture hierarchical compositional representations remains a central challenge in statistical learning theory. We introduce Brownian kernel ladders (BKLs), a recursively defined hierarchy of integral reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces generated through Brownian-kernel integral constructions. Starting from linear functionals, each layer is obtained by integrating Brownian kernels over probability measures supported on subsets of the previous layer, yielding a recursive function-space model in which depth is encoded directly through the hierarchy.
  Based on this framework, we define canonical BKL spaces together with an associated complexity functional. We establish several analytical and statistical properties of these spaces. In particular, we show that BKL spaces form quasi-Banach spaces, satisfy depth-dependent Hölder regularity estimates, and exhibit strict monotonicity with respect to depth. We further prove existence results for regularized empirical risk minimization and derive Gaussian complexity bounds that remain uniformly controlled with respect to both the ambient dimension and the hierarchy depth.
  A key ingredient of the analysis is a combinatorial proof technique based on recursive subset decompositions and Brownian-kernel threshold representations. These estimates yield excess-risk guarantees of near-parametric order for regularized empirical risk minimization over BKL spaces. Our results provide a mathematically tractable hierarchical function-space framework for studying compositional representations in deep learning.

AI.tldr: 本文提出布朗核阶梯（BKLs），一种基于布朗核积分构造的递归定义的再生核希尔伯特空间层次结构，用于建模分层组合表示，并建立了其分析与统计性质，包括准巴拿赫空间结构、深度相关的Hölder正则性、单调性及泛化误差界。
AI.motivation: 构建能数学刻画分层组合表示的可处理函数空间是统计学习理论的核心挑战。
AI.method: 引入布朗核阶梯（BKLs），通过在前一层子集上的概率测度上对布朗核积分递归构造函数空间；定义典型BKL空间及相应复杂度泛函；采用递归子集分解与布朗核阈值表示的组合证明技术。
AI.result: 证明BKL空间构成准巴拿赫空间，满足深度依赖的Hölder正则性估计和严格深度单调性；给出带正则项的经验风险最小化的存在性结果；导出关于环境维数与层次深度均一致可控的高斯复杂度界及近参数阶的超额风险保证。
AI.conclusion: BKL框架为深度学习中组合表示的理论研究提供了数学上可处理的分层函数空间基础。

title: When Should a Robot Replan? Regret-Guided Update Scheduling in Time-Varying MDPs
summary: Robots operating in non-stationary environments must continually adapt their policies as the dynamics drift, but onboard energy and compute budgets cap how often a full state estimation and re-planning step can be performed. This raises a question: \emph{when}, along a horizon, should a robot spend its limited budget? We formulate this problem in time-varying Markov decision processes (TVMDPs) with a known bound on the rate of transition drift. We model execution as a \emph{skip-update} scheme in which, at chosen update times, the agent estimates the transition kernel by maximum likelihood and computes a finite-horizon policy, and between updates reuses this policy under a propagated state estimate. We analyze the dynamic regret of this scheme and show how it grows during skip intervals in terms of the properties of the TVMDP and the skip lengths; the resulting bound answers the opening question via an online, regret-guided update rule that allocates the budget adaptively. We evaluate the rule in a simulated Mars-rover navigation task with time-varying slip dynamics and on a Crazyflie quadrotor in indoor obstacle fields. Adaptive allocation outperforms other budgeted baselines.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种在时变马尔可夫决策过程（TVMDP）中自适应分配有限计算与估计预算的skip-update机制，通过动态遗憾分析指导何时更新策略，在火星车仿真和Crazyflie无人机实验中验证了其优于固定频率等基线方法。
AI.motivation: 非平稳环境中机器人需持续适应策略，但受限于机载能源与算力，无法频繁执行完整状态估计与重规划；因此需解决‘何时更新’这一预算分配问题。
AI.method: 在具有已知转移漂移率上界的TVMDP框架下，提出skip-update执行机制：在选定更新时刻通过最大似然估计转移核并计算有限视界策略，其余时间复用该策略并传播状态估计；基于动态遗憾分析推导跳过区间内的性能衰减，并据此设计在线、遗憾引导的自适应更新规则。
AI.result: 理论推导出动态遗憾随跳过长度和TVMDP性质增长的上界；在火星车滑移动力学仿真和Crazyflie室内障碍环境实验中，自适应预算分配显著优于固定间隔、周期性等基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 动态遗憾可作为预算分配的可靠指标，所提出的在线自适应更新规则能在资源受限下实现更优长期性能，为非平稳环境中的实时机器人决策提供了理论支持与实用方案。

title: CausalDrive: Real-time Causal World Models for Autonomous Driving
summary: World models have emerged as a promising paradigm for scaling autonomous driving (AD) data, yet existing video generative models fall short as interactive simulators. Layout-conditioned renderers rely on "oracle" future trajectories of all background agents, rendering them strictly non-reactive. Conversely, pure action-conditioned predictors lack semantic control over complex interactions and suffer from prohibitive diffusion latencies, hindering closed-loop policy learning. To bridge this gap, we present CausalDrive, a controllable, real-time foundation driving world renderer. CausalDrive operates solely on the initial front-view frame, the ego-vehicle's trajectory, and a macroscopic text prompt. By excluding future NPC layouts, we compel the model to intrinsically predict causal interactions, enabling text-driven control over Driving Sociology, allowing users to dynamically orchestrate diverse counterfactual reactions to identical ego-actions. To overcome the efficiency bottleneck and address the covariate shift in autoregressive generation, we propose a novel Context-Forced DMD architecture. This combines continuous flow-matching with a self-correcting distillation objective, achieving interactive speeds of 12 FPS. This breakthrough transforms the passive video generator into a playable neural simulator. We demonstrate its versatility across three downstream applications: (1) generative closed-loop evaluation with significantly mitigated collision artifacts, (2) large-scale Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training driven by a Video2Reward module, and (3) real-time human-in-the-loop simulation. Extensive experiments validate that policies trained within CausalDrive's reactive scenarios exhibit superior interaction capabilities in the real world.

AI.tldr: 本文提出CausalDrive，一种可控、实时的驾驶世界渲染器，通过仅依赖初始图像、自车轨迹和文本提示，实现对交通场景中因果交互的建模与文本驱动控制，并结合Context-Forced DMD架构达成12 FPS实时性能，支持闭环评估、强化学习训练和人在环仿真。
AI.motivation: 现有视频生成模型无法作为交互式驾驶模拟器：布局条件渲染器依赖未来真值轨迹而缺乏反应性；动作条件预测器缺乏语义控制且推理慢，难以支持闭环策略学习。
AI.method: 提出CausalDrive框架，摒弃对未来NPC布局的依赖，仅用初始前视图、自车轨迹和宏观文本提示进行生成；设计Context-Forced DMD架构，融合连续流匹配与自校正蒸馏目标，提升效率与鲁棒性。
AI.result: 实现12 FPS实时生成；在生成式闭环评估、视频到奖励驱动的强化学习、实时人在环仿真三类下游任务中验证有效性；在真实世界测试中，于CausalDrive中训练的策略展现出更优交互能力。
AI.conclusion: CausalDrive将被动视频生成器转变为可交互的神经模拟器，首次实现了文本驱动、因果感知、实时响应的驾驶世界建模，为数据高效、安全可靠的自动驾驶开发提供了新范式。

title: Surpassing Scale by Efficiency: A Compact 135M Parameter Foundational LLM Natively Adapted for the Bangla Language
summary: While the NLP landscape is dominated by multi-billion parameter architectures, their deployment in low-resource, non-Latin scripts remains computationally prohibitive for edge configurations, mobile systems, and decentralized local hardware. This paper presents bangla-smollm-135m, a highly compact 135-million parameter decoder-only foundational model engineered explicitly for high-efficiency language modeling in the Bangla script. By leveraging a deterministic intersect-and-append token merging strategy between TituLLMs and SmolLM2-135M, the model overcomes subword script fragmentation without destabilizing early pretrained parameter states. In zero-shot multi-task benchmark evaluations (PIQA_bn, OpenBookQA_bn, CommonsenseQA_bn, and Bangla_MMLU), bangla-smollm-135m matches or outperforms models twice its size (Gemma-3-270m) and achieves parity with models in the 1B parameter tier. The model is available at rnnandi/bangla-smollm-135m

AI.tldr: 本文提出bangla-smollm-135m，一个专为孟加拉语设计的1.35亿参数高效语言模型，通过创新的token合并策略解决子词分割问题，在零样本多任务基准上性能媲美更大模型。
AI.motivation: 解决大型语言模型在低资源、非拉丁文字（如孟加拉语）场景下难以部署于边缘设备、移动端和本地硬件的问题。
AI.method: 采用确定性的交集-追加（intersect-and-append）token合并策略，融合TituLLMs与SmolLM2-135M，兼顾脚本适配与预训练参数稳定性。
AI.result: 在PIQA_bn、OpenBookQA_bn、CommonsenseQA_bn和Bangla_MMLU等零样本多任务基准中，性能持平或超越2.7亿参数的Gemma-3-270m，并达到约10亿参数模型水平。
AI.conclusion: bangla-smollm-135m验证了针对特定脚本轻量化建模的有效性，为低资源语言的高效本地化部署提供了可行路径。

title: Post-Hoc Merging is Not Enough: Many-Shot Model Merging with Loss-Gap Balancing
comment: Accepted to the 43rd International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2026)
summary: Model merging has become a practical post-training strategy for building a single multi-task large language model (LLM) by combining multiple task-specialized models. However, most existing approaches rely on post-hoc merging, in which task-specific models are merged only once after training. This one-shot aggregation often suffers from task interference, leading to information erasure across individual tasks. In this work, we show that replacing post-hoc merging with an iterative many-shot merging protocol is effective in improving multi-task performance. Building on this insight, we propose METIS, Mitigating Erasure from Task Interference for Stable many-shot merging. METIS is a loss-aware many-shot merging method that addresses information erasure in post-hoc merging through task-wise loss-gap weighting and consensus-based masking. Notably, METIS exhibits significant performance improvement on the worst-performing task, effectively mitigating information erasure. (Project page: https://imkyungjin.github.io/METIS/)

AI.tldr: 本文提出METIS方法，通过多轮迭代合并多个任务专用模型，以缓解后验合并中因任务干扰导致的信息擦除问题，显著提升最差任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有模型合并方法多为一次性后验合并，易引发任务间干扰和信息擦除，影响多任务性能。
AI.method: 提出METIS方法，采用损失感知的多轮合并策略，结合任务级损失差距加权与基于共识的掩码机制。
AI.result: METIS在最差任务上表现出显著性能提升，有效缓解信息擦除问题。
AI.conclusion: 多轮迭代合并优于一次性后验合并，METIS通过损失感知机制提升了多任务LLM的稳定性和整体性能。

title: SILAGE: Memory-Efficient, Full-Gradient-Free Nonconvex Optimization for Nested Finite Sums
comment: 80 pages, 3 algorithms, 4 theorems, 2 corollaries, 11 lemmas, 2 figures, 12 tables
summary: Empirical risk minimization on massive datasets naturally exhibits a nested double finite-sum structure, where $N=nm$ total samples are logically or physically partitioned into $n$ blocks of size $m$ (e.g., in pooled data silos, out-of-core learning, or deliberate stratification). While variance-reduced methods achieve optimal oracle complexities for nonconvex objectives, they suffer from severe scaling bottlenecks in this centralized regime. Recursive estimators, such as PAGE, require periodic global full-gradient refreshes over all $nm$ samples, which are computationally expensive. Conversely, single-loop methods, such as SILVER, avoid such refreshes but require an impractical $\mathcal{O}(nm)$ memory footprint to store a control variate for every sample.
